reasoning: At least three individuals whom police believe were physically present and directly involved in carrying out the October 19th Louvre robbery have been arrested and formally charged as of late October/early November 2025. DNA evidence links at least one of these primary suspects to the crime scene, and reports indicate that certain suspects have 'partially admitted their involvement.' Although some suspects (including a girlfriend of one of the men and accomplices) have been released without charge or under judicial supervision, the main perpetrators remain in custody. The case fulfills the key requirements of the market: apprehension of at least one physical perpetrator before December 25, 2025, with no consensus of exonerating reporting within a month. Given the strength of the evidence (DNA and partial confessions) and the passage of nearly a month since the primary arrests without exoneration, the probability of a YES resolution is overwhelmingly high. The market estimate of 99% prior to this date further verifies this certainty. I align my forecast at the maximum allowable bound of 0.95.
